title: stenocalyx dysentericus
intro: Stenocalyx dysentericus, also known as the Mexican oak, is a plant species native to Mexico and Central America, belonging to the family Rutaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been found to be beneficial in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Stenocalyx dysentericus include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
